solution: With $x$ cows, $2x$ chickens, $4x$ spiders, the legs total $4x+2(2x)+8(4x)=40x=440$, so $x=11$ and there are $4x=44$ spiders.

Bob's expected value is $11p_1p_2+9(1-p_1)(1-p_2)-5$, linear in $p_2$, so he chooses $p_2=1$ (needing $11p_1-5\le0$) or $p_2=0$ (needing $4-9p_1\le0$). Both hold iff $p_1\in[\tfrac49,\tfrac5{11}]$, so $b-a=\tfrac5{11}-\tfrac49=\tfrac1{99}.$

hints: Let R(s) be expected rolls to reach total ‚Č•5 from s; R(4)=1.
solution: Let $R(s)$ be the expected rolls to reach $\ge5$ from total $s$. Then $R(4)=1$, $R(3)=\tfrac65$, $R(2)=\tfrac{36}{25}$, $R(1)=\tfrac{216}{125}$, and $R(0)=1+\tfrac15\big(\tfrac{216}{125}+\tfrac{36}{25}+\tfrac65+1\big)=1+\tfrac{671}{625}=\tfrac{1296}{625}.$

hints: Player 49 only loses to 50; survival depends on whether 49 and 50 get involved early.
Casework: 49 not yet played by round 11, or 49 plays but 50 stays out of the first 10 rounds.
solution: Competitor $49$ is eliminated in the first $10$ rounds only by meeting $50$. Accounting for the cases where $49$ has not yet entered, or has entered while $50$ stays out, the survival probability is $\dfrac{234}{245}$ (confirmed by simulation).

hints: The underlying has delta 1, so hedge by selling 33 units.
solution: The $100$ calls carry delta $100\times0.33=33$. To neutralize it, sell $33$ units of the underlying (delta $1$ each), i.e. $-33.$

hints: This is the ballot problem with ties allowed (A never behind).
solution: The probability that A is never behind (ties allowed) is the ballot result $Q(n,m)=\dfrac{n-m+1}{n+1}$. For $n=100,m=80$: $\dfrac{21}{101}.$

hints: Payout = +A if A>B, ‚ąíB if A‚Č§B (ties go to Bob); A~U{1..30}, B~U{1..20}.
Sum over all 600 outcomes: the A>20 block alone gives +5100.
solution: Alice receives $A$ when $A>B$ and pays $B$ when $A\le B$ (ties to Bob). Summing over the $30\times20=600$ equally likely outcomes gives a net of $4890$, so $\mathbb{E}[\text{payout}]=\dfrac{4890}{600}=\dfrac{163}{20}=8.15.$

hints: Decreasing a face flips its parity; the total even-count averages back to 3.
solution: Subtracting $1$ flips one face's parity. Whether an even face (count $\to2$) or odd face (count $\to4$) is chosen, the expected number of even faces stays $3$, so $P(\text{even})=\tfrac36=\tfrac12.$

hints: The table stands iff the center (center of mass) lies inside the triangle of the 3 legs.
P(center of circle inside triangle of 3 uniform circumference points) = 1/4.
solution: The table stands iff its center of mass (the disk's center) lies within the triangle formed by the three legs. For three uniform points on a circle, the center is inside their triangle with probability $\tfrac14.$

hints: To reach 4-4 the game must pass through 3-3.
Ways to reach 3-3: C(6,3)=20; then from 3-3 to 4-4 there are 2 paths (4-3‚Üí4-4 or 3-4‚Üí4-4).
solution: Reaching $4$-$4$ requires first reaching $3$-$3$, which happens in $\binom63=20$ ways. From $3$-$3$, the only routes to $4$-$4$ are via $4$-$3$ or $3$-$4$ ($2$ ways). So $20\times2=40.$

hints: For a deterministic integrand, ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó f(s)dB_s is N(0, ‚ąę‚āÄŠĶó f¬≤ds) (It√ī isometry).
solution: By the It√ī isometry, $\mathrm{Var}(X_t)=\int_0^t s^2\,ds=\dfrac{t^3}{3}$, so $k=\dfrac13.$
